sense.definition: a small, often brightly colored cone used to mark boundaries or specific areas on a field in sports like soccer and rugby

examples.x: The coach placed a cone marker at each corner of the field.
During practice, we had to dribble around the cone markers.
The players lined up behind the cone marker for their sprint drills.
We used cone markers to set up a mini soccer field in the park.
The rugby training session included passing drills around cone markers.

sense.definition: a small stand used to hold the ball in place for the starting kick in American football

examples.x: During practice, they used the tee to perfect their kickoff technique.
The coach brought extra tees to the practice session.
Every football game begins with the ball placed on a tee for the kickoff.
The football flew off the tee as he delivered a powerful kick.

sense.definition: a small pin used to hold a golf ball off the ground for the first stroke of each hole

examples.x: The golfer placed the ball on the tee before taking his shot.
During the game, she adjusted the height of her tee for a better drive.
He broke the tee with his powerful drive, sending the ball flying.
The golf course provided free tees for all players.
After the shot, he picked up the tee and placed it back in his pocket.

sense.definition: a sports equipment used in baseball or softball to hold a ball stationary for a player to hit

examples.x: The coach set up the batting tee for practice.
The batting tee is essential for beginners in baseball.
He knocked the ball off the batting tee with a powerful hit.
They carried the batting tee to the field for drills.
After each hit, he placed another ball on the batting tee.

sense.definition: a small, rectangular slab in baseball and softball from which the pitcher throws the ball

examples.x: The pitcher stepped onto the pitching rubber, ready to throw the first pitch.
The pitching rubber is exactly 60 feet 6 inches from home plate.
He adjusted his grip on the ball while standing on the pitching rubber.
During practice, she focused on her stance on the pitching rubber.
Rain made the pitching rubber slippery, affecting his performance.

sense.definition: (cricket) the set of three sticks with two small sticks on top that the bowler tries to hit and the batsman tries to protect

examples.x: The batsman defended the wicket from the fast ball.
The bowler aimed at the wicket to try and get the batsman out.
Each cricket field has two wickets at opposite ends.
They celebrated taking the wicket during the match.
The umpire checked the wicket to make sure it was set up right before the game.

sense.definition: a large, usually white, screen used in sports such as cricket to provide a clear background against which the ball can be seen by the batsman

examples.x: The cricket ground had a sight screen placed behind the bowler's arm.
The sight screen was adjusted to minimize glare from the setting sun.
A sight screen is essential for clear visibility of the ball during daytime matches.
The team captain instructed the fielders to keep away from the sight screen during play.
The sight screen was brought closer to the pitch as the light faded.

sense.definition: a container used for collecting and carrying sports balls

examples.x: The tennis coach used a ball hopper to collect the tennis balls after practice.
The basketball team's manager carried a ball hopper onto the court to gather stray basketballs.
She emptied the ball hopper and refilled it with fresh balls for the next drill.
The ball hopper made it easy for the players to retrieve their tennis balls during training.
The team's equipment manager ordered a new ball hopper to replace the old, worn-out one.

sense.definition: a  specialized equipment used in powerlifting and Olympic weightlifting for stable weightlifting

examples.x: The gym installed a new weightlifting platform for athletes.
John practiced deadlifts on the weightlifting platform.
The competition venue had Olympic-style weightlifting platforms.
Olympic weightlifting platforms are typically made of durable wood or rubber materials.
The dimensions of a standard weightlifting platform ensure enough space for lifting barbells safely.

sense.definition: a hanging stuffed bag that is often used for practicing punching and striking techniques in boxing and martial arts

examples.x: The boxer spent hours training with the punching bag to perfect his jabs and hooks.
Hitting the punching bag is a great way to release stress and improve upper body strength.
She installed a punching bag in her garage to practice kickboxing at home.
The coach demonstrated the proper technique for striking the punching bag without injuring one's wrists.
The heavy punching bag swayed back and forth with each powerful punch from the athlete.

sense.definition: a padded target used in combat sports training, held by a trainer or partner for striking practice

examples.x: The boxer practiced his combinations on the focus mitt with his coach.
The MMA fighter used the focus mitt to simulate real fight scenarios.
She improved her speed and accuracy by hitting the focus mitt during training sessions.
The kickboxer's coach wore focus mitts to absorb powerful kicks during practice.
In Muay Thai, focus mitts are essential for practicing knee and elbow strikes.

sense.definition: a small, inflated punching bag used primarily in boxing and martial arts training to improve hand-eye coordination and punching technique

examples.x: The boxer practiced his jabs and hooks on the speed bag.
She hit the speed bag with precise rhythm and control.
Training on the speed bag helps fighters develop faster hand speed.
The coach instructed him to maintain a steady pace while hitting the speed bag.
Learning to time punches with the speed bag is crucial for boxing technique.

sense.definition: a sports equipment used primarily in martial arts to absorb impact from kicks and strikes

examples.x: The coach held the kick pad steady while the student practiced front kicks.
The kick pad was made of durable foam to withstand intense training sessions.
He practiced his roundhouse kicks on the kick pad to improve his technique.
The gym had a variety of kick pads for different types of martial arts training.
Fighters use kick pads during sparring sessions to simulate real combat scenarios.

sense.definition: a sports equipment used primarily in wrestling training to simulate an opponent's body

examples.x: The wrestling team practiced takedowns on the wrestling dummy.
He improved his grappling techniques by working with a wrestling dummy daily.
The wrestler pinned the wrestling dummy effortlessly.
She used the wrestling dummy to perfect her throws and holds.
Wrestlers often warm up by practicing moves on the wrestling dummy.

sense.definition: a gear used in climbing to secure climbers to the rock

examples.x: John placed a climbing anchor before starting the climb.
The guide taught us to build a climbing anchor with nuts.
They used a climbing anchor to rappel safely.
Climbers chose bolts for the climbing anchor.
Building a climbing anchor needs rock knowledge.

sense.definition: a flexible, springy platform often made of fiberglass or wood, used in swimming pools for diving into the water

examples.x: The diver bounced on the diving board before executing a perfect dive.
Every pool equipped for diving has a sturdy diving board installed at one end.
The lifeguard monitored the pool area near the diving board to ensure safety.
During the competition, she performed a series of dives from the high diving board.
The diving board's non-slip surface prevented accidents during dives.

sense.definition: a platform with footholds used by sprinters and swimmers at the beginning of a race to push off with maximum force and speed

examples.x: The sprinter crouched on the starting block, ready for the race to begin.
During the competition, she exploded off the starting block to gain an early lead.
She focused on her breathing and technique while setting up on the starting block.
The starting block's design allows athletes to push off with strong momentum.
He practiced his starts repeatedly, refining his technique on the starting block.

sense.definition: a sports equipment used primarily in swimming to display elapsed time for training or competition purposes

examples.x: The pace clock guided swimmers in their interval training.
Swimmers checked the pace clock after each lap.
Coaches adjusted the workout based on the pace clock readings.
The pace clock helped swimmers maintain consistent speed.
Pace clocks are essential equipment in competitive swimming.

sense.definition: a marked surface or object that shooters aim at to score points or achieve accuracy in shooting competitions

examples.x: The shooter focused intensely on hitting the target during the competition.
During practice, she adjusted her aim to hit the center of the target consistently.
Every shooting range provides various targets for different disciplines and distances.
The target's bullseye is the highest-scoring area and requires precise shooting.
He aimed carefully and squeezed the trigger to hit the target accurately.

sense.definition: a buoyant rectangular float used in swimming, primarily for support or as a training aid

examples.x: He held onto the kickboard while floating on his back in the pool.
She practiced her kicking technique with a kickboard during swim practice.
The lifeguard handed out kickboards to the beginners in the swimming class.
Sarah forgot her kickboard, so she borrowed one from a friend.
The swimmer left his kickboard by the edge of the pool after finishing his workout.

sense.definition: a piece of sports equipment used in swimming to increase resistance, strength, and endurance

examples.x: The swim parachute slowed down his laps, making each stroke more challenging.
Using a swim parachute in practice helps build muscle and technique.
She attached the swim parachute to her waist before starting the swim set.
Athletes wear swim parachutes to develop power and stamina in the water.
Adjusting to the resistance of the swim parachute, he focused on maintaining form.

sense.definition: a flotation device used in swimming to increase buoyancy and isolate the upper body

examples.x: Using a pull buoy can help swimmers develop a more efficient stroke.
Swimmers often use a pull buoy to focus on improving their arm stroke technique.
Competitive swimmers include pull buoys in their training routines for strength building.
During the drill, the instructor demonstrated proper form while holding a pull buoy.
When swimming long distances, many athletes find a pull buoy reduces fatigue in their legs.

sense.definition: the specialized resistance socks used in swimming to enhance strength and technique

examples.x: Swimmers wear DragSox to increase resistance during training sessions.
DragSox help improve leg strength and endurance in the water.
Many competitive swimmers use DragSox to enhance their performance.
Training with DragSox can lead to faster times in races.

sense.definition: a gymnastics equipment consisting of two horizontal bars set parallel to each other, used for performing various acrobatic and strength exercises

examples.x: Every gymnastics gym is equipped with parallel bars for athletes to train on.
The gymnast swung gracefully between the parallel bars, showcasing her skills.
The gymnast's routine on the parallel bars earned high scores from the judges.
He admired the gymnasts' strength and flexibility while watching them on the parallel bars.
At the gymnastics meet, spectators cheered as the gymnasts performed on the parallel bars.

sense.definition: a narrow, elevated equipment typically covered in padded material, used by gymnasts for performing balance routines and acrobatic skills

examples.x: During practice, she focused on perfecting her balance and precision on the beam.
The beam's padded surface provides cushioning for landings and support during routines.
She walked confidently across the length of the beam, demonstrating her control.
The beam's height and width are standardized for competitive gymnastics events.

sense.definition: a gymnastics apparatus consisting of two horizontal bars set at different heights, used primarily by female gymnasts for performing acrobatic routines involving swings, releases, and transitions

examples.x: The gymnast showcased her strength and agility on the uneven bars during the competition.
During training, she practiced intricate transitions and dismounts on the uneven bars.
The uneven bars' design allows for dynamic and high-flying routines.
She adjusted her grips and swings to maximize her routine's difficulty on the uneven bars.
The coach provided feedback on the gymnast's form and technique on the uneven bars.

sense.definition: a gymnastics apparatus consisting of bars, beams, and other equipment used for training and performing routines

examples.x: The gymnast practiced on the rig to perfect her balance beam routine.
He swung gracefully from one bar to another on the rig.
The coach adjusted the height of the rig for the gymnasts' training session.
During competition, the gymnasts showcased their skills on the rig.
He struggled with the rings on the rig but eventually mastered the routine.

sense.definition: a piece of gymnastics equipment with two handles on its surface by which gymnasts can perform particular moves on

examples.x: The gymnast performed a flawless routine on the pommel horse, showcasing his strength and agility.
Training on the pommel horse requires a significant amount of upper body strength and coordination.
During the competition, he lost his grip on the pommel horse and had to restart his routine.
The coach adjusted the height of the pommel horse to match the gymnast's preferred settings.
Mastering the pommel horse is considered one of the most challenging aspects of men's gymnastics.

sense.definition: a padded platform used in gymnastics for athletes to jump off and perform acrobatic moves during the vault event

examples.x: The gymnast practiced her routine on the vaulting horse to improve her flips and landings.
He ran down the runway and pushed off the vaulting horse, soaring through the air.
After a few attempts, she finally stuck the landing after jumping off the vaulting horse.
He felt nervous the first time he tried the vaulting horse, but it became easier with practice.
The gymnasts lined up to take turns jumping off the vaulting horse during their training session.

sense.definition: a bar fixed horizontally above the ground for used for various exercises requiring gripping and pulling motions

examples.x: Gymnasts practice their routines on the horizontal bar, showcasing their strength and agility.
The fitness instructor demonstrated pull-ups on the horizontal bar as part of the workout session.
Children enjoy playing on the horizontal bar at the playground, developing their coordination and strength.
The athlete performed a flawless routine on the horizontal bar, impressing the judges with his skill.

sense.definition: a surface with built-in springs to enhance rebound and cushioning in gymnastics

examples.x: The gymnasts practiced their tumbling routines on the spring floor.
She landed her back handspring perfectly on the spring floor.
The cheerleaders performed their stunts with confidence on the spring floor.
They rolled out the mats over the spring floor for safety during practice.
His favorite part of gymnastics class was using the spring floor for flips and jumps.

sense.definition: one of the three vertical wooden posts that are hammered into the ground at each end of the pitch, with two small horizontal bails resting on top

examples.x: The bowler knocked over the stumps to celebrate taking a wicket.
The batsman defended his stumps carefully against the fast deliveries.
Every cricket field has stumps set up at both ends for bowling and batting.
The wicketkeeper stood behind the stumps to catch the ball.
He celebrated with teammates after hitting the stumps with a direct throw.

sense.definition: one of the three stumps or the vertical wooden posts that form part of the wicket in cricket, used to determine if a batsman is out

examples.x: The umpire raised his finger as the ball crashed into the leg stump.
The bowler knocked over the leg stump with a yorker delivery.
The batsman was bowled out when the ball hit the leg stump.
The batsman defended well, keeping the ball away from the leg stump.
The fielding team celebrated as the ball deflected off the leg stump onto the stumps.

sense.definition: a horizontal bar suspended by ropes or cables, used by acrobats for performing aerial maneuvers and swings

examples.x: The acrobat gracefully swung from one end of the trapeze to the other, thrilling the audience with their daring stunts.
As a skilled performer, she executed breathtaking flips and somersaults on the trapeze with effortless grace.
Acrobats train extensively to master the techniques required to perform on the trapeze, including balance, timing, and coordination.
With each swing on the trapeze, the acrobat displayed remarkable precision and control, captivating the audience with their daring aerial displays.

sense.definition: the circular ring with a net attached, mounted on a backboard, through which players aim to score points by shooting a basketball

examples.x: The player aimed carefully and shot the ball through the basketball hoop.
During practice, they took turns shooting at the basketball hoop from different angles.
The new basketball hoop was installed with a sturdy backboard.
He practiced dunking the ball into the basketball hoop.
They cheered when the ball soared through the basketball hoop for a three-pointer.

sense.definition: an obstacle in steeplechase races in athletics, consisting of a water pit that athletes must jump over after hurdling barriers

examples.x: He cleared the water jump effortlessly in the steeplechase race.
The water jump adds an element of challenge and excitement to the steeplechase.
He trained specifically to improve his form over the water jump.
She emerged from the water jump and continued her strong pace
He stumbled slightly after landing in the water jump but quickly recovered.

sense.definition: a mechanical device used at the beginning of horse races to ensure a fair start

examples.x: The horses were loaded into the starting gate for the race.
The jockeys lined up their mounts inside the starting gate.
The starting gate ensures a controlled and fair start to horse races.
Each horse was positioned carefully in the starting gate to avoid collisions.
The starting gate opened, and the race began with a thunderous roar.

sense.definition: a bouncy platform that helps people jump higher, often seen in pools and gymnastics arenas

examples.x: She bounced on the springboard before diving into the pool.
The gymnast performed a perfect flip off the springboard.
He adjusted the springboard to get the right amount of bounce.
The kids took turns jumping off the springboard into the water.
She used the springboard to gain extra height for her dive.
